name: Cael
description: Cael Ua Neamhnainn, a figure of immense strength and courage, is a prominent warrior from Irish mythology, primarily known for his tragic love affair with Creide and his valiant death in battle. He was renowned as one of the most handsome and skilled warriors of the Fianna, a band of elite warriors led by Fionn mac Cumhaill. His lineage traces back to the Uí Néill, a powerful dynasty in ancient Ireland, further solidifying his status and importance. Cael's prowess in combat was unmatched, and he was often called upon to lead the Fianna in their most dangerous expeditions. He was not only a fierce warrior but also possessed a gentle heart, evident in his deep love for Creide. Their love story is a poignant tale of devotion and sacrifice, highlighting the complexities of love amidst the chaos of war. Cael's death in the Battle of Ventry, while defending his honor and the honor of the Fianna, cemented his place as a tragic hero in Irish folklore. His legacy lives on through the stories and poems that celebrate his bravery, loyalty, and the enduring power of love, even in the face of death. His tale serves as a reminder of the sacrifices made by the heroes of old and the enduring impact of their deeds on Irish culture and identity. He embodies the ideals of courage, chivalry, and unwavering commitment, making him a timeless figure in Irish mythology.
subtitle: Handsome warrior, tragic love, heroic death
achievements: Defending the honor of the Fianna
Becoming a symbol of tragic heroism

keyMyths.title: The Love of Cael and Creide
keyMyths.description: Cael and Creide fell deeply in love, but their happiness was short-lived.  Their romance is a central and tragic element in the narratives surrounding Cael.

keyMyths.title: Cael's Death at the Battle of Ventry
keyMyths.description: Cael met his end at the Battle of Ventry, a major conflict in Irish legend. He fought valiantly before succumbing to his wounds, a testament to his bravery.

keyMyths.title: Cael's Beauty and Strength
keyMyths.description: Cael was renowned for his exceptional beauty and unmatched strength. These qualities made him a formidable warrior and a desirable suitor.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: A tale detailing the love story of Cael and Creide and the events leading to their tragic fate.
